From Wikipedia, the free encyclopedia

For other uses, see [Red carpet (disambiguation)](/wiki/Red_carpet_(disambiguation) "Red carpet (disambiguation)").

Marking route used for arrivals of dignitaries

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/5/5a/Pi%C3%B1era_Paris_arrival_2010.jpg/250px-Pi%C3%B1era_Paris_arrival_2010.jpg)](/wiki/File:Pi%C3%B1era_Paris_arrival_2010.jpg)

[Chilean President](/wiki/President_of_Chile "President of Chile") [Sebastián Piñera](/wiki/Sebasti%C3%A1n_Pi%C3%B1era "Sebastián Piñera") arriving in [Paris](/wiki/Paris "Paris") in October 2010

A **red carpet** is traditionally used to mark the route taken by [heads of state](/wiki/Heads_of_state "Heads of state") on ceremonial and formal occasions, and has in recent decades been extended to use by [VIPs](/wiki/Very_Important_Person "Very Important Person") and [celebrities](/wiki/Celebrity "Celebrity") at formal events.

## History

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Red_carpet&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: History")]

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/0/04/Barack_Obama_leaving_Air_Force_One_by_night.jpg/250px-Barack_Obama_leaving_Air_Force_One_by_night.jpg)](/wiki/File:Barack_Obama_leaving_Air_Force_One_by_night.jpg)

[United States President](/wiki/President_of_the_United_States "President of the United States") [Barack Obama](/wiki/Barack_Obama "Barack Obama") exiting [Air Force One](/wiki/Air_Force_One "Air Force One") on red carpet

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/4/45/Halle_Berry_red_carpet.jpg/250px-Halle_Berry_red_carpet.jpg)](/wiki/File:Halle_Berry_red_carpet.jpg)

[Halle Berry](/wiki/Halle_Berry "Halle Berry") attending the premiere of the movie *[Robots](/wiki/Robots_(2005_film) "Robots (2005 film)")*

The earliest known reference to walking a red carpet in literature is in the play *[Agamemnon](/wiki/The_Oresteia "The Oresteia")* by [Aeschylus](/wiki/Aeschylus "Aeschylus"), written in 458 BC. When the title character returns from Troy, he is greeted by his wife [Clytemnestra](/wiki/Clytemnestra "Clytemnestra") who offers him a red path to walk upon:

> Now, dearest husband, come, step from your chariot.  
> But do not set to earth, my lord, the conquering foot  
> That trod down Troy. Servants, do as you have been bidden;  
> Make haste, carpet his way with crimson tapestries,  
> Spread silk before your master's feet; Justice herself  
> Shall lead him to a home he never hoped to see.[[1]](#cite_note-Aeschylus1973-1)

Agamemnon, knowing that only [gods](/wiki/Greek_mythology "Greek mythology") walk on such luxury, responds with trepidation:

> I count it dangerous, being mortal, to set foot  
> On rich embroidered silks. I would be reverenced  
> As man, not god. The praise of fame rings clear without  
> These frills and fancy foot-rugs;[[1]](#cite_note-Aeschylus1973-1)

[Renaissance paintings often show rugs and carpets in oriental patterns](/wiki/Oriental_carpets_in_Renaissance_painting "Oriental carpets in Renaissance painting"), with red often the main background colour, laid on the steps to a throne, or on a dais where rulers or sacred figures are placed.

A red carpet was rolled out to welcome the arrival of United States president [James Monroe](/wiki/James_Monroe "James Monroe") in South Carolina in 1821.[[2]](#cite_note-2) In 1902, the [New York Central Railroad](/wiki/New_York_Central_Railroad "New York Central Railroad") began using plush crimson carpets to direct people as they boarded their *[20th Century Limited](/wiki/20th_Century_Limited "20th Century Limited")* passenger train. This is believed to be the origin of the phrase "red-carpet treatment".[[3]](#cite_note-3)

By the late 1920s the red carpet had become synonymous with [Hollywood](/wiki/Cinema_of_the_United_States "Cinema of the United States") and film [premieres](/wiki/Premiere "Premiere"). A crimson-hued carpet was used for the first ever Hollywood premiere, the 1922 premiere of *[Robin Hood](/wiki/Douglas_Fairbanks_in_Robin_Hood "Douglas Fairbanks in Robin Hood")*, starring [Douglas Fairbanks](/wiki/Douglas_Fairbanks "Douglas Fairbanks"), in front of the [Egyptian Theatre](/wiki/Grauman%27s_Egyptian_Theatre "Grauman's Egyptian Theatre"). For the following decades, the red carpet was one of the few places the public could catch a glimpse of stars like [Clark Gable](/wiki/Clark_Gable "Clark Gable"), [Jimmy Stewart](/wiki/Jimmy_Stewart "Jimmy Stewart") and [Grace Kelly](/wiki/Grace_Kelly "Grace Kelly").[[4]](#cite_note-auto-4)

In 1961, the red carpet was introduced at the [Academy Awards](/wiki/Academy_Awards "Academy Awards") at the [Santa Monica Civic Auditorium](/wiki/Santa_Monica_Civic_Auditorium "Santa Monica Civic Auditorium"). In 1964, the broadcasters of the ceremony opted to film outside the venue, showing the arrival of guests as they stepped out of their limousines. From this point forward, the red carpet became a globally acknowledged focal point for actors and actresses to make a grand entrance and showcase themselves at the Oscars.[[4]](#cite_note-auto-4)

## The entertainment industry and publicity events

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Red_carpet&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: The entertainment industry and publicity events")]

For at least 4 decades, a red carpet, often called the "Hollywood red carpet" has been regularly used in gala celebrity and entertainment events such as the [Academy Awards](/wiki/Academy_Awards "Academy Awards"), [Golden Globe Awards](/wiki/Golden_Globe_Awards "Golden Globe Awards"), [Grammy Awards](/wiki/Grammy_Awards "Grammy Awards"), [Met Gala](/wiki/Met_Gala "Met Gala"), and [BAFTAs](/wiki/British_Academy_of_Film_and_Television_Arts "British Academy of Film and Television Arts"). While the awards ceremonies take place inside the venues, much of the publicity occurs outside, with journalists discussing the [red carpet fashions](/wiki/Red_carpet_fashion "Red carpet fashion") and which famous [fashion designers](/wiki/Fashion_designers "Fashion designers") are being worn by the celebrities. Numerous photo-journalists are always present in the [press line](/wiki/Press_line "Press line"). The "Hollywood red carpet" has become an important [product placement](/wiki/Product_placement "Product placement") arena for the fashion industry, and it is often coupled with [publicity backdrops](/wiki/Step_and_repeat "Step and repeat") which contain brand logos or emblems for photography and advertisement purposes.[[5]](#cite_note-5)

### Other colors

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Red_carpet&action=edit&section=3 "Edit section: Other colors")]

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/1/1b/Adam_Jones_on_Opening_Day_2018_%2841104580191%29.jpg/250px-Adam_Jones_on_Opening_Day_2018_%2841104580191%29.jpg)](/wiki/File:Adam_Jones_on_Opening_Day_2018_(41104580191).jpg)

[Adam Jones](/wiki/Adam_Jones_(baseball) "Adam Jones (baseball)") of the [Baltimore Orioles](/wiki/Baltimore_Orioles "Baltimore Orioles") jogs down an orange carpet on [Opening Day](/wiki/Opening_Day "Opening Day") at [Camden Yards](/wiki/Camden_Yards "Camden Yards") in 2018. Orange is the team's primary color.

Carpeting in other colors may replace red in some instances to honor a certain cause, such as a "green carpet" to promote environmental awareness; or, for a sponsored event, the sponsor's logo colors, such as the [Nickelodeon Kids' Choice Awards](/wiki/Nickelodeon_Kids%27_Choice_Awards "Nickelodeon Kids' Choice Awards"), which uses an orange carpet to match the network's primary imaging color;[[6]](#cite_note-6) The [MTV Video Music Awards](/wiki/MTV_Video_Music_Awards "MTV Video Music Awards") have similarly varied, with some editions having used black ([2022](/wiki/2022_MTV_Video_Music_Awards "2022 MTV Video Music Awards")),[[7]](#cite_note-7) white ([2016](/wiki/2016_MTV_Video_Music_Awards "2016 MTV Video Music Awards")),[[8]](#cite_note-8) and blue ([2017](/wiki/2017_MTV_Video_Music_Awards "2017 MTV Video Music Awards")) carpets.[[9]](#cite_note-9)

In 2019, the premiere of the film *[Detective Pikachu](/wiki/Detective_Pikachu_(film) "Detective Pikachu (film)")* (2019) used a yellow carpet to match the color of its title character [Pikachu](/wiki/Pikachu "Pikachu").[[10]](#cite_note-10) The premiere of *[Sonic the Hedgehog](/wiki/Sonic_the_Hedgehog_(film) "Sonic the Hedgehog (film)")* (2020) used a blue carpet to match its [namesake character](/wiki/Sonic_the_Hedgehog_(character) "Sonic the Hedgehog (character)").[[11]](#cite_note-11)

At the [95th Academy Awards](/wiki/95th_Academy_Awards "95th Academy Awards") in 2023, the organizers chose a different [champagne](/wiki/Champagne_(color) "Champagne (color)") color for the first time since the red carpet was introduced, as part of an attempt to better reflect a "day event into the night".[[12]](#cite_note-12)

The *[Jeopardy! Honors](/wiki/List_of_Jeopardy!_tournaments_and_events "List of Jeopardy! tournaments and events")* event, which was held annually since Season 39 on the eve of the *Tournament of Champions*, featured a blue carpet, in order to match the color of its background used during clues and dollar amounts shown on the clue board, from inside the Alex Trebek Stage at [Sony Pictures Studios](/wiki/Sony_Pictures_Studios "Sony Pictures Studios").

## Phrases

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Red_carpet&action=edit&section=4 "Edit section: Phrases")]

More generally, "red carpet treatment" and "rolling out the red carpet" usually refer to any special efforts made in the interests of [hospitality](/wiki/Hospitality "Hospitality").[*[citation needed](/wiki/Wikipedia:Citation_needed "Wikipedia:Citation needed")*]

## Airline lounges

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Red_carpet&action=edit&section=5 "Edit section: Airline lounges")]

[United Airlines](/wiki/United_Airlines "United Airlines") operates a series of [airport lounges](/wiki/Airport_lounge "Airport lounge") at major airports that were formerly known as "[Red Carpet Clubs](/wiki/Red_Carpet_Club "Red Carpet Club")".[[13]](#cite_note-United_Club-13)
